extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. The danger is transition t4: on send_attempt, if (opted_out and acc==45), it sets sent:=true while opted_out is true. So the property holds only if acc==45 is unreachable. It is reachable. Each step event computes acc := (3*acc + c) mod 48: step_0 uses c=5, step_1 uses c=27, step_2 uses c=11 (the triple-nested if-then-else subtracts 48 up to three times, which exactly equals mod 48 since the max value 3*47+27=168 needs at most three subtractions; verified by exhaustive check over acc in [0,47]). Starting from acc=0: step_1 -> (0+27)%48=27; step_0 -> (81+5)%48=86%48=38; step_1 -> (114+27)%48=141%48=45. Now acc=45 with opted_out=false, sent=false. Then optout sets opted_out=true, sent=false (still satisfies property). Then send_attempt: guards t2 (not opted_out) fails, t3 (opted_out and acc!=45) fails since acc==45, so t4 (opted_out and acc==45) fires, setting sent:=true. Resulting state has opted_out=true AND sent=true, violating opted_out implies not sent. The 5-event execution is within the 50-event bound. Simulation confirms VIOLATION=True at the final state.
